content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest

916 322-5662        322-5660         322-5901         322-5441         322-5444


September 15, 1983


Lance H. Olson
Olson, Connelly & Hagel
431 J Street, Fourth Floor
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: FPPC Advice No. A-83-202

Dear Lance:

     This is in reply to your letter dated July 25, 1983, regarding
the verification requirement for copies of campaign statements and
reports required under the Political Reform Act.

     The staff's advice is that when copies of campaign disclosure
statements and reports are required to be filed, if they are not
photocopies which bear the signature and verification date, the
copies must contain the typed, written, stamped or printed name of
the filer in the space in which the signature appears on the
original. In addition, copies must include the full date (month,
day and year) on which the original was verified by the filer.

     In your letter of July 25, 1983, you asked that the staff
reconsider this advice or, in the alternative, that the matter be
brought to the Commission for a ruling. The staff has reconsidered
this advice and believes that the purposes of the Political Reform
Act are best served by the requirement that all copies of
statements and reports required to be filed comply with the method
of verification set out above. The Commission has reviewed your
letter and this response and has approved the staff advice.

     Government Code Section 81004 requires that all statements and
reports filed under the Political Reform Act be "signed under
penalty of perjury and verified by the filer." Filing officers who
do not receive original statements have duties under the Act to
determine if the copies they receive conform with all the
requirements of the Act, including the requirement of a signature
and verification. If a filing officer receives a copy of a
statement or report that does not adequately indicate that it has
been properly verified by the filer, the officer would be unable to
determine whether the filing conforms with the Act's requirements.

Lance Olson
September 16, 1983
Page 2


     We are aware that some inconvenience arises when a person
completing statements for a candidate or committee is located in
another city from the filer, so that the statement must be mailed
to the filer for verification. However, it cannot be too difficult
to arrange to have the name of the filer and the verification date
typed on the copies prior to signing, if it is too burdensome to
Xerox the signature page before filing the copies.

     Thank you for your inquiry. Please contact me if you have any
questions.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        [illegible]
                                        Jeanne Pritchard
                                        Political Reform Consultant

LAW OFFICES OF

OLSON, CONNELLY & HAGEL
431 J STREET, FOURTH FLOOR
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814

BRUCE J. HAGEL
LANCE H. OLSON

OF COUNSEL
LLOYD S. CONNELLY

TELEPHONE (916) 443-2952

July 25, 1983

Ms. Barbara Milman
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re: Advice Letter A-82-009

Dear Barbara:

I am writing this letter regarding two advice staff
letters which appear to require some indication on copies
of campaign reports that the original has been signed and
filed. As I understand the current staff interpretation
of Government Code Section 81004, all copies must bear the
typed, written, stamped or printed name of the signature
which appears on the original. In addition the date the
original was signed must also appear on the copies.

I would like to take exception to this interpretation
and request that the staff reconsider this advice or in
and the alternative that the matter be brought to the attention
of the commission for a ruling.

It is my position that there is no requirement that
the date and name appear on the copies. I would submit
that a copy of the report without the verification completed
or with the words "original signed" (which this office has
consistently used) meets the requirements of the Act.

I would appreciate your comments and suggestions.

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY & HAGEL

LANCE H. OLSON

LHO/kl

cc John Hodgson
Dobbs & Nielsen

State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 307 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 X STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance · · Administration · · Executive/Legal · · Enforcement · · Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           322-5901        322-6441        322-3444

November 15, 1931

Joanne Paiock
Registrar of Voters
P.O. Box 11298
Santa Ana, CA  92711

Dear Joanne:

This is in reply to your recent inquiry concerning the filing of copies
of campaign statements which do not bear the signature of the filer.

The staff has reviewed the advice letter dated July 21, 1975, from
Van Hambleton of the Commission's staff, to Richard Bryant, and has
confirmed the advice provided in that letter.

Gov. Code Section 81004 provides that all statements and reports must
be signed and verified by the filer. Although copies of campaign statements do not have to bear the signature appearing on an
original campaign statement, the requirement that copies be verified
for accuracy by the filer applies. To indicate that the original has
been verified and signed, and that the unsigned copies have been filed,
the name of the filer should be typed, written, stamped or printed
in the same space in which the signature appears on the original. We
suggest that copies of campaign statements be conformed in the same
manner as copies of legal documents are conformed. i.e.:

by: ___/s/ JOHN DOE, TREASURER____
(signature of Committee treasurer or
individual donor)

Regarding the date of verification, it is not sufficient to indicate
merely the month and year that the verification was made. As the execution
date is a part of the verification, the full date, including the month,
day and year, that the verification is made must be shown.

Please call me if you have any further questions.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Jeanne Pritchard
Political Reform Consultant

    Donna & [illegible]

sections.question: s.
sections.conclusion: The staff's advice is that when copies of campaign disclosure statements and reports are required to be filed, if they are not photocopies which bear the signature and verification date, the copies must contain the typed, written, stamped or printed name of the filer in the space in which the signature appears on the original. In addition, copies must include the full date (month, day and year) on which the original was verified by the filer. The staff has reconsidered this advice and believes that the purposes of the Political Reform Act are best served by the requirement that all copies of statements and reports required to be filed comply with the method of verification set out above. The Commission has reviewed your letter and this response and has approved the staff advice.
sections.question_synthetic: Are campaign report copies required to display the filer's typed or printed name and full verification date, or can they simply indicate "original signed" without this information?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC affirms that all copies of campaign statements must contain the typed, written, stamped or printed name of the filer and the full date of verification (month, day, year) where the signature appears, and that simply marking "original signed" does not meet the Act's requirements.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Complete advice letter with both incoming request and FPPC response sections. OCR quality is good. Document includes three related pieces: the primary FPPC response (September 16, 1983), the incoming request letter (July 25, 1983), and a related earlier advice letter from November 1981 (A-82-009 referenced). All three sections extracted successfully.

In addition, copies must include the full date (month, day and year) on which the original was verified by the filer. The staff has reconsidered this advice and believes that the purposes of the Political Reform Act are best served by the requirement that all copies of statements and reports required to be filed comply with the method of verification set out above. embedding.summary: FPPC Advice Letter A-83-202 addresses the verification requirements for copies of campaign disclosure statements under the Political Reform Act, holding that copies must display the filer's name and full verification date.
